items.title: Fit scalers on training data only
items.avoid: Computing global mean, std, min, or max on concatenated train+test matrices before splitting.
items.prefer: scaler.fit(X_train) then transform all splits, or nest StandardScaler inside Pipeline with CV.
items.why: Preprocessing statistics must not observe holdout distributions.

items.title: Match scaler to algorithm family
items.avoid: One-size-fits-all MinMax on every project regardless of model.
items.prefer: StandardScaler for k-NN, k-means, PCA, SVM, Ridge/Lasso, NNs; MinMax when bounded inputs are required; no scaler for tree-only baselines.
items.why: Each learner uses features differently—distance, gradients, or order statistics.
items.example: Pipeline([('scale', StandardScaler()), ('knn', KNeighborsClassifier())]) vs bare RandomForestClassifier on raw X.

items.title: Serialize scaler with the model
items.avoid: Fitting StandardScaler in a notebook cell separate from the saved estimator.
items.prefer: Single Pipeline or ColumnTransformer saved via joblib containing both scaler and model.
items.why: Production and audits need identical transforms at inference.
items.example: joblib.dump(pipe, 'churn_knn.joblib') where pipe includes the fitted StandardScaler.

items.title: Inspect distributions before MinMax
items.avoid: Blind MinMax on skewed monetary columns with rare huge values.
items.prefer: Plot train histograms; consider log1p, winsorize, or StandardScaler when tails are heavy.
items.why: MinMax bounds are entirely determined by train min/max and are outlier-sensitive.
items.example: After log1p on `revenue`, MinMax maps the majority of SMB accounts across more of [0, 1].
